event_name: GlassWASM: WebAssembly Malware Found in Trojanized Open VSX Extensions
confidence_reason: Socket directly analyzed the malicious Open VSX artifacts and documented the WebAssembly loader, Solana memo dead-drop, and second-stage execution chain. The link to GlassWorm is medium-confidence attribution, but the incident itself is well supported.

credentials_at_risk: CI secrets reachable from the affected host
cloud credentials reachable from the affected host
wallet or blockchain-development secrets reachable from the affected host

artifact_analysis.execution_trigger: the extension auto-executes a TinyGo WebAssembly payload on activation via an appended bootstrap and onStartupFinished hook
payload_behavior: instantiates a TinyGo-compiled WebAssembly module through a JavaScript host
queries Solana mainnet JSON-RPC for transactions to an attacker-controlled wallet
extracts memo data from SPL Memo instructions to resolve the current C2 host
builds OS-specific curl or PowerShell download-and-execute commands
spawns the command through child_process.execSync with windowsHide enabled

provenance.registry_state: Open VSX API detail and query endpoints now return not found or empty results for both affected package/version pairs

filesystem_hunts: Open VSX or VS Code-fork extension inventories containing exargd/vsblack@0.0.1 or noellee-doc/flint-debug@0.1.1
extension trees or telemetry mentioning orybbbdsuqmaapel.wasm or snqpkebiwrxmoivl.wasm
process_hunts: node or IDE helper processes invoking child_process.execSync with curl pipe-to-bash or irm pipe-to-iex patterns
runtime traces containing getSignaturesForAddress followed by getTransaction jsonParsed requests
network_hunts: api.mainnet.solana.com requests from Node.js or VS Code extension contexts that should not query Solana
ci_cd_hunts: not_applicable unless the affected IDE host or developer workstation also serves as a build runner
registry_hunts: Open VSX API detail and query results for exargd/vsblack and noellee-doc/flint-debug returning not found or empty results after removal

open_questions: Socket says the originals remained clean on the VS Code Marketplace, but no direct Open VSX metadata snapshot older than the removal state was recoverable in this run.
The operator's final second-stage payload was not publicly recovered because dodod.lat did not serve it at analysis time.
The direct compromise status of the original GitHub repositories or publisher namespaces remains not observed in the reviewed sources.

defender_takeaways.detection: Hunt for the two Open VSX package/version pairs, the zaitoona43 account, the Solana wallet and memo program IDs, dodod.lat, and node or IDE helper processes that turn blockchain memo data into child_process.execSync download commands.
defender_takeaways.hunting: Collect extension inventories, extension directories, exported process telemetry, and network logs before removing the packages because the second stage can rotate independently of the shipped artifact.
defender_takeaways.remediation: Uninstall the trojanized extensions from Open VSX-backed editors, preserve artifacts, and rotate every developer or cloud credential reachable from a host that executed the loader.
defender_takeaways.prevention: Treat WebAssembly plus a tiny JavaScript loader in marketplace extensions as a high-signal review condition, especially when the package mirrors a legitimate upstream extension across registries.

containment_complete: The two trojanized Open VSX package/version pairs are removed from managed editor fleets
dodod.lat is blocked or alerted on at network controls where appropriate
eradication_complete: Residual extension directories and caches are removed after evidence capture
No IDE helper or node process continues to query api.mainnet.solana.com for the watched wallet
recovery_complete: Developer, CI, cloud, and wallet secrets reachable from executing hosts are rotated from a clean environment
Rebuilt or verified-clean editors are redeployed
closure_required: No matched hosts still contain the affected extension IDs or wasm filenames
No process or network telemetry still shows the Solana dead-drop or dodod.lat retrieval patterns
Risk owner accepts the remaining uncertainty about the unrecovered second stage
